chapters.zh: 所罗门的歌，是歌中的雅歌。（新娘）
chapters.en: The song of songs, which Solomon's.

chapters.zh: 愿他用口与我亲嘴；因你的爱情比酒更美。
chapters.en: Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth: for thy love better than wine.

chapters.zh: 你的膏油馨香；你的名如同倒出来的香膏，所以众童女都爱你。
chapters.en: Because of the savour of thy good ointments thy name ointment poured forth, therefore do the virgins love thee.

chapters.zh: 愿你吸引我，我们就快跑跟随你。王带我进了内室，我们必因你欢喜快乐。我们要称赞你的爱情，胜似称赞美酒。他们爱你是理所当然的。
chapters.en: Draw me, we will run after thee: the king hath brought me into his chambers: we will be glad and rejoice in thee, we will remember thy love more than wine: the upright love thee.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊，我虽然黑，却是秀美，如同基达的帐棚，好像所罗门的幔子。
chapters.en: I black, but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, as the tents of Kedar, as the curtains of Solomon.

chapters.zh: 不要因日头把我晒黑了就轻看我。我同母的弟兄向我发怒，他们使我看守葡萄园；我自己的葡萄园却没有看守。
chapters.en: Look not upon me, because I black, because the sun hath looked upon me: my mother's children were angry with me; they made me the keeper of the vineyards; mine own vineyard have I not kept.

chapters.zh: 我心所爱的啊，求你告诉我，你在何处牧羊？晌午在何处使羊歇卧？我何必在你同伴的羊群旁边好像蒙著脸的人呢？（新郎）
chapters.en: Tell me, O thou whom my soul loveth, where thou feedest, where thou makest to rest at noon: for why should I be as one that turneth aside by the flocks of thy companions?

chapters.zh: 你这女子中极美丽的，你若不知道，只管跟随羊群的脚踪去，把你的山羊羔牧放在牧人帐棚的旁边。
chapters.en: If thou know not, O thou fairest among women, go thy way forth by the footsteps of the flock, and feed thy kids beside the shepherds' tents.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶，我将你比法老车上套的骏马。
chapters.en: I have compared thee, O my love, to a company of horses in Pharaoh's chariots.

chapters.zh: 你的两腮因发辫而秀美；你的颈项因珠串而华丽。
chapters.en: Thy cheeks are comely with rows , thy neck with chains .

chapters.zh: 我们要为你编上金辫，镶上银钉。（新娘）
chapters.en: We will make thee borders of gold with studs of silver.

chapters.zh: 王正坐席的时候，我的哪哒香膏发出香味。
chapters.en: While the king at his table, my spikenard sendeth forth the smell thereof.

chapters.zh: 我以我的良人为一袋没药，常在我怀中。
chapters.en: A bundle of myrrh my wellbeloved unto me; he shall lie all night betwixt my breasts.

chapters.zh: 我以我的良人为一棵凤仙花，在隐基底葡萄园中。（新郎）
chapters.en: My beloved unto me a cluster of camphire in the vineyards of Engedi.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶，你甚美丽！你甚美丽！你的眼好像鸽子眼。（新娘）
chapters.en: Behold, thou fair, my love; behold, thou fair; thou doves' eyes.

chapters.zh: 我的良人哪，你甚美丽可爱！我们以青草为床榻，
chapters.en: Behold, thou fair, my beloved, yea, pleasant: also our bed green.

chapters.zh: 以香柏树为房屋的栋梁，以松树为椽子。
chapters.en: The beams of our house cedar, our rafters of fir.

chapters.zh: 我是沙仑的玫瑰花（或译：水仙花），是谷中的百合花。（新郎）
chapters.en: I the rose of Sharon, the lily of the valleys.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶在女子中，好像百合花在荆棘内。（新娘）
chapters.en: As the lily among thorns, so my love among the daughters.

chapters.zh: 我的良人在男子中，如同苹果树在树林中。我欢欢喜喜坐在他的荫下，尝他果子的滋味，觉得甘甜。
chapters.en: As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit sweet to my taste.

chapters.zh: 他带我入筵宴所，以爱为旗在我以上。
chapters.en: He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me love.

chapters.zh: 求你们给我葡萄干增补我力，给我苹果畅快我心，因我思爱成病。
chapters.en: Stay me with flagons, comfort me with apples: for I sick of love.

chapters.zh: 他的左手在我头下；他的右手将我抱住。
chapters.en: His left hand under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊，我指著羚羊或田野的母鹿嘱咐你们：不要惊动、不要叫醒我所亲爱的，等他自己情愿（不要叫醒 … … 情愿：或译不要激动爱情，等他自发）。
chapters.en: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake love, till he please.

chapters.zh: 听啊！是我良人的声音；看哪！他蹿山越岭而来。
chapters.en: The voice of my beloved! behold, he cometh leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.

chapters.zh: 我的良人好像羚羊，或像小鹿。他站在我们墙壁后，从窗户往里观看，从窗櫺往里窥探。
chapters.en: My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.

chapters.zh: 我良人对我说：（新郎）我的佳偶，我的美人，起来，与我同去！
chapters.en: My beloved spake, and said unto me, Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.

chapters.zh: 因为冬天已往，雨水止住过去了。
chapters.en: For, lo, the winter is past, the rain is over gone;

chapters.zh: 地上百花开放，百鸟鸣叫的时候（或译：修理葡萄树的时候）已经来到；斑鸠的声音在我们境内也听见了。
chapters.en: The flowers appear on the earth; the time of the singing is come, and the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;

chapters.zh: 无花果树的果子渐渐成熟；葡萄树开花放香。我的佳偶，我的美人，起来，与我同去！
chapters.en: The fig tree putteth forth her green figs, and the vines the tender grape give a smell. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.

chapters.zh: 我的鸽子啊，你在磐石穴中，在陡岩的隐密处。求你容我得见你的面貌，得听你的声音；因为你的声音柔和，你的面貌秀美。
chapters.en: O my dove, in the clefts of the rock, in the secret of the stairs, let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; for sweet thy voice, and thy countenance comely.

chapters.zh: 要给我们擒拿狐狸，就是毁坏葡萄园的小狐狸，因为我们的葡萄正在开花。（新娘）
chapters.en: Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vines: for our vines tender grapes.

chapters.zh: 良人属我，我也属他；他在百合花中牧放群羊。
chapters.en: My beloved mine, and I his: he feedeth among the lilies.

chapters.zh: 我的良人哪，求你等到天起凉风、日影飞去的时候，你要转回，好像羚羊，或像小鹿在比特山上。
chapters.en: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be thou like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.

chapters.zh: 我夜间躺卧在床上，寻找我心所爱的；我寻找他，却寻不见。
chapters.en: By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

chapters.zh: 我说：我要起来，游行城中，在街市上，在宽阔处，寻找我心所爱的。我寻找他，却寻不见。
chapters.en: I will rise now, and go about the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I will seek him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

chapters.zh: 城中巡逻看守的人遇见我；我问他们：你们看见我心所爱的没有？
chapters.en: The watchmen that go about the city found me: , Saw ye him whom my soul loveth?

chapters.zh: 我刚离开他们就遇见我心所爱的。我拉住他，不容他走，领他入我母家，到怀我者的内室。
chapters.en: but a little that I passed from them, but I found him whom my soul loveth: I held him, and would not let him go, until I had brought him into my mother's house, and into the chamber of her that conceived me.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊，我指著羚羊或田野的母鹿嘱咐你们：不要惊动、不要叫醒我所亲爱的，等他自己情愿（不要叫醒 … … 情愿：或译不要激动爱情，等他自发）。
chapters.en: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake love, till he please.

chapters.zh: 那从旷野上来、形状如烟柱、以没药和乳香并商人各样香粉薰的是谁呢？
chapters.en: Who this that cometh out of the wilderness like pillars of smoke, perfumed with myrrh and frankincense, with all powders of the merchant?

chapters.zh: 看哪，是所罗门的轿；四围有六十个勇士，都是以色列中的勇士；
chapters.en: Behold his bed, which Solomon's; threescore valiant men about it, of the valiant of Israel.

chapters.zh: 手都持刀，善于争战，腰间佩刀，防备夜间有惊慌。
chapters.en: They all hold swords, expert in war: every man his sword upon his thigh because of fear in the night.

chapters.zh: 所罗门王用利巴嫩木为自己制造一乘华轿。
chapters.en: King Solomon made himself a chariot of the wood of Lebanon.

chapters.zh: 轿柱是用银做的，轿底是用金做的；坐垫是紫色的，其中所铺的乃耶路撒冷众女子的爱情。
chapters.en: He made the pillars thereof silver, the bottom thereof gold, the covering of it purple, the midst thereof being paved love, for the daughters of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 锡安的众女子啊，你们出去观看所罗门王！头戴冠冕，就是在他婚筵的日子、心中喜乐的时候，他母亲给他戴上的。
chapters.en: Go forth, O ye daughters of Zion, and behold king Solomon with the crown wherewith his mother crowned him in the day of his espousals, and in the day of the gladness of his heart.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶，你甚美丽！你甚美丽！你的眼在帕子内好像鸽子眼。你的头发如同山羊群卧在基列山旁。
chapters.en: Behold, thou fair, my love; behold, thou fair; thou doves' eyes within thy locks: thy hair as a flock of goats, that appear from mount Gilead.

chapters.zh: 你的牙齿如新剪毛的一群母羊，洗净上来，个个都有双生，没有一只丧掉子的。
chapters.en: Thy teeth like a flock shorn, which came up from the washing; whereof every one bear twins, and none barren among them.

chapters.zh: 你的唇好像一条朱红线；你的嘴也秀美。你的两太阳在帕子内，如同一块石榴。
chapters.en: Thy lips like a thread of scarlet, and thy speech comely: thy temples like a piece of a pomegranate within thy locks.

chapters.zh: 你的颈项好像大卫建造收藏军器的高台，其上悬挂一千盾牌，都是勇士的籐牌。
chapters.en: Thy neck like the tower of David builded for an armoury, whereon there hang a thousand bucklers, all shields of mighty men.

chapters.zh: 你的两乳好像百合花中吃草的一对小鹿，就是母鹿双生的。
chapters.en: Thy two breasts like two young roes that are twins, which feed among the lilies.

chapters.zh: 我要往没药山和乳香冈去，直等到天起凉风、日影飞去的时候回来。
chapters.en: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶，你全然美丽，毫无瑕疵！
chapters.en: Thou all fair, my love; no spot in thee.

chapters.zh: 我的新妇，求你与我一同离开利巴嫩，与我一同离开利巴嫩。从亚玛拿顶，从示尼珥与黑门顶，从有狮子的洞，从有豹子的山往下观看。
chapters.en: Come with me from Lebanon, spouse, with me from Lebanon: look from the top of Amana, from the top of Shenir and Hermon, from the lions' dens, from the mountains of the leopards.

chapters.zh: 我妹子，我新妇，你夺了我的心。你用眼一看，用你项上的一条金炼，夺了我的心！
chapters.en: Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, spouse; thou hast ravished my heart with one of thine eyes, with one chain of thy neck.

chapters.zh: 我妹子，我新妇，你的爱情何其美！你的爱情比酒更美！你膏油的香气胜过一切香品！
chapters.en: How fair is thy love, my sister, spouse! how much better is thy love than wine! and the smell of thine ointments than all spices!

chapters.zh: 我新妇，你的嘴唇滴蜜，好像蜂房滴蜜；你的舌下有蜜，有奶。你衣服的香气如利巴嫩的香气。
chapters.en: Thy lips, O spouse, drop the honeycomb: honey and milk under thy tongue; and the smell of thy garments like the smell of Lebanon.

chapters.zh: 我妹子，我新妇，乃是关锁的园，禁闭的井，封闭的泉源。
chapters.en: A garden inclosed my sister, spouse; a spring shut up, a fountain sealed.

chapters.zh: 你园内所种的结了石榴，有佳美的果子，并凤仙花与哪哒树。
chapters.en: Thy plants an orchard of pomegranates, with pleasant fruits; camphire, with spikenard,

chapters.zh: 有哪哒和番红花，菖蒲和桂树，并各样乳香木、没药、沉香，与一切上等的果品。
chapters.en: Spikenard and saffron; calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense; myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices:

chapters.zh: 你是园中的泉，活水的井，从利巴嫩流下来的溪水。（新娘）
chapters.en: A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, and streams from Lebanon.

chapters.zh: 北风啊，兴起！南风啊，吹来！吹在我的园内，使其中的香气发出来。愿我的良人进入自己园里，吃他佳美的果子。
chapters.en: Awake, O north wind; and come, thou south; blow upon my garden, the spices thereof may flow out. Let my beloved come into his garden, and eat his pleasant fruits.

chapters.zh: 我妹子，我新妇，我进了我的园中，采了我的没药和香料，吃了我的蜜房和蜂蜜，喝了我的酒和奶。（耶路撒冷的众女子）我的朋友们，请吃！我所亲爱的，请喝，且多多地喝！
chapters.en: I am come into my garden, my sister, spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.

chapters.zh: 我身睡卧，我心却醒。这是我良人的声音；他敲门说：（新郎）我的妹子，我的佳偶，我的鸽子，我的完全人，求你给我开门；因我的头满了露水，我的头发被夜露滴湿。（新娘）
chapters.en: I sleep, but my heart waketh: the voice of my beloved that knocketh, , Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is filled with dew, my locks with the drops of the night.

chapters.zh: 我回答说：我脱了衣裳，怎能再穿上呢？我洗了脚，怎能再玷污呢？
chapters.en: I have put off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?

chapters.zh: 我的良人从门孔里伸进手来，我便因他动了心。
chapters.en: My beloved put in his hand by the hole , and my bowels were moved for him.

chapters.zh: 我起来，要给我良人开门。我的两手滴下没药；我的指头有没药汁滴在门闩上。
chapters.en: I rose up to open to my beloved; and my hands dropped myrrh, and my fingers sweet smelling myrrh, upon the handles of the lock.

chapters.zh: 我给我的良人开了门；我的良人却已转身走了。他说话的时候，我神不守舍；我寻找他，竟寻不见；我呼叫他，他却不回答。
chapters.en: I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.

chapters.zh: 城中巡逻看守的人遇见我，打了我，伤了我；看守城墙的人夺去我的披肩。
chapters.en: The watchmen that went about the city found me, they smote me, they wounded me; the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊，我嘱咐你们：若遇见我的良人，要告诉他，我因思爱成病。（耶路撒冷的众女子）
chapters.en: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I sick of love.

chapters.zh: 你这女子中极美丽的，你的良人比别人的良人有何强处？你的良人比别人的良人有何强处，你就这样嘱咐我们？（新娘）
chapters.en: What thy beloved more than beloved, O thou fairest among women? what thy beloved more than beloved, that thou dost so charge us?

chapters.zh: 我的良人白而且红，超乎万人之上。
chapters.en: My beloved white and ruddy, the chiefest among ten thousand.

chapters.zh: 他的头像至精的金子；他的头发厚密累垂，黑如乌鸦。
chapters.en: His head the most fine gold, his locks bushy, black as a raven.

chapters.zh: 他的眼如溪水旁的鸽子眼，用奶洗净，安得合式。
chapters.en: His eyes as of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, fitly set.

chapters.zh: 他的两腮如香花畦，如香草台；他的嘴唇像百合花，且滴下没药汁。
chapters.en: His cheeks as a bed of spices, sweet flowers: his lips lilies, dropping sweet smelling myrrh.

chapters.zh: 他的两手好像金管，镶嵌水苍玉；他的身体如同雕刻的象牙，周围镶嵌蓝宝石。
chapters.en: His hands gold rings set with the beryl: his belly bright ivory overlaid sapphires.

chapters.zh: 他的腿好像白玉石柱，安在精金座上；他的形状如利巴嫩，且佳美如香柏树。
chapters.en: His legs pillars of marble, set upon sockets of fine gold: his countenance as Lebanon, excellent as the cedars.

chapters.zh: 他的口极其甘甜；他全然可爱。耶路撒冷的众女子啊，这是我的良人；这是我的朋友。
chapters.en: His mouth most sweet: yea, he altogether lovely. This my beloved, and this my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 你这女子中极美丽的，你的良人往何处去了？你的良人转向何处去了，我们好与你同去寻找他。（新娘）
chapters.en: Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside? that we may seek him with thee.

chapters.zh: 我的良人下入自己园中，到香花畦，在园内牧放群羊，采百合花。
chapters.en: My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the beds of spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.

chapters.zh: 我属我的良人，我的良人也属我；他在百合花中牧放群羊。
chapters.en: I my beloved's, and my beloved mine: he feedeth among the lilies.

chapters.zh: 我的佳偶啊，你美丽如得撒，秀美如耶路撒冷，威武如展开旌旗的军队。
chapters.en: Thou beautiful, O my love, as Tirzah, comely as Jerusalem, terrible as with banners.

chapters.zh: 求你掉转眼目不看我，因你的眼目使我惊乱。你的头发如同山羊群卧在基列山旁。
chapters.en: Turn away thine eyes from me, for they have overcome me: thy hair as a flock of goats that appear from Gilead.

chapters.zh: 你的牙齿如一群母羊洗净上来，个个都有双生，没有一只丧掉子的。
chapters.en: Thy teeth as a flock of sheep which go up from the washing, whereof every one beareth twins, and not one barren among them.

chapters.zh: 你的两太阳在帕子内，如同一块石榴。
chapters.en: As a piece of a pomegranate thy temples within thy locks.

chapters.zh: 有六十王后八十妃嫔，并有无数的童女。
chapters.en: There are threescore queens, and fourscore concubines, and virgins without number.

chapters.zh: 我的鸽子，我的完全人，只有这一个是他母亲独生的，是生养他者所宝爱的。众女子见了就称他有福；王后妃嫔见了也赞美他。
chapters.en: My dove, my undefiled is one; she the one of her mother, she the choice of her that bare her. The daughters saw her, and blessed her; , the queens and the concubines, and they praised her.

chapters.zh: 那向外观看、如晨光发现、美丽如月亮、皎洁如日头、威武如展开旌旗军队的是谁呢？
chapters.en: Who she looketh forth as the morning, fair as the moon, clear as the sun, terrible as with banners?

chapters.zh: 我下入核桃园，要看谷中青绿的植物，要看葡萄发芽没有，石榴开花没有。
chapters.en: I went down into the garden of nuts to see the fruits of the valley, to see whether the vine flourished, the pomegranates budded.

chapters.zh: 不知不觉，我的心将我安置在我尊长的车中。（耶路撒冷的众女子）
chapters.en: Or ever I was aware, my soul made me the chariots of Amminadib.

chapters.zh: 回来，回来，书拉密女；你回来，你回来，使我们得观看你。（新娘）你们为何要观看书拉密女，像观看玛哈念跳舞的呢？
chapters.en: Return, return, O Shulamite; return, return, that we may look upon thee. What will ye see in the Shulamite? As it were the company of two armies.

chapters.zh: 王女啊，你的脚在鞋中何其美好！你的大腿圆润，好像美玉，是巧匠的手做成的。
chapters.en: How beautiful are thy feet with shoes, O prince's daughter! the joints of thy thighs like jewels, the work of the hands of a cunning workman.

chapters.zh: 你的肚脐如圆杯，不缺调和的酒；你的腰如一堆麦子，周围有百合花。
chapters.en: Thy navel a round goblet, wanteth not liquor: thy belly an heap of wheat set about with lilies.

chapters.zh: 你的两乳好像一对小鹿，就是母鹿双生的。
chapters.en: Thy two breasts like two young roes twins.

chapters.zh: 你的颈项如象牙台；你的眼目像希实本、巴特拉并门旁的水池；你的鼻子彷佛朝大马色的利巴嫩塔。
chapters.en: Thy neck as a tower of ivory; thine eyes the fishpools in Heshbon, by the gate of Bathrabbim: thy nose as the tower of Lebanon which looketh toward Damascus.

chapters.zh: 你的头在你身上好像迦密山；你头上的发是紫黑色；王的心因这下垂的发绺系住了。
chapters.en: Thine head upon thee like Carmel, and the hair of thine head like purple; the king held in the galleries.

chapters.zh: 我所爱的，你何其美好！何其可悦，使人欢畅喜乐！
chapters.en: How fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!

chapters.zh: 你的身量好像棕树；你的两乳如同其上的果子，累累下垂。
chapters.en: This thy stature is like to a palm tree, and thy breasts to clusters .

chapters.zh: 我说：我要上这棕树，抓住枝子。愿你的两乳好像葡萄累累下垂，你鼻子的气味香如苹果；
chapters.en: I said, I will go up to the palm tree, I will take hold of the boughs thereof: now also thy breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the smell of thy nose like apples;

chapters.zh: 你的口如上好的酒。（新娘）女子说：为我的良人下咽舒畅，流入睡觉人的嘴中。
chapters.en: And the roof of thy mouth like the best wine for my beloved, that goeth sweetly, causing the lips of those that are asleep to speak.

chapters.zh: 我属我的良人，他也恋慕我。
chapters.en: I my beloved's, and his desire toward me.

chapters.zh: 我的良人，来吧！你我可以往田间去；你我可以在村庄住宿。
chapters.en: Come, my beloved, let us go forth into the field; let us lodge in the villages.

chapters.zh: 我们早晨起来往葡萄园去，看看葡萄发芽开花没有，石榴放蕊没有；我在那里要将我的爱情给你。
chapters.en: Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see if the vine flourish, the tender grape appear, the pomegranates bud forth: there will I give thee my loves.

chapters.zh: 风茄放香，在我们的门内有各样新陈佳美的果子；我的良人，这都是我为你存留的。
chapters.en: The mandrakes give a smell, and at our gates all manner of pleasant , new and old, I have laid up for thee, O my beloved.

chapters.zh: 巴不得你像我的兄弟，像吃我母亲奶的兄弟；我在外头遇见你就与你亲嘴，谁也不轻看我。
chapters.en: O that thou as my brother, that sucked the breasts of my mother! I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; yea, I should not be despised.

chapters.zh: 我必引导你，领你进我母亲的家；我可以领受教训，也就使你喝石榴汁酿的香酒。
chapters.en: I would lead thee, bring thee into my mother's house, would instruct me: I would cause thee to drink of spiced wine of the juice of my pomegranate.

chapters.zh: 他的左手必在我头下；他的右手必将我抱住。
chapters.en: His left hand under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊，我嘱咐你们：不要惊动、不要叫醒我所亲爱的，等他自己情愿（不要叫醒 … … 情愿：或译不要激动爱情，等他自发）。
chapters.en: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, that ye stir not up, nor awake love, until he please.

chapters.zh: 那靠著良人从旷野上来是谁呢？（新娘）我在苹果树下叫醒你。你母亲在的那里为你劬劳；生养你的在那里为你劬劳。
chapters.en: Who this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved? I raised thee up under the apple tree: there thy mother brought thee forth: there she brought thee forth bare thee.

chapters.zh: 求你将我放在你心上如印记，带在你臂上如戳记。因为爱情如死之坚强，嫉恨如阴间之残忍；所发的电光是火焰的电光，是耶和华的烈焰。
chapters.en: Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love strong as death; jealousy cruel as the grave: the coals thereof coals of fire, most vehement flame.

chapters.zh: 爱情，众水不能息灭，大水也不能淹没。若有人拿家中所有的财宝要换爱情，就全被藐视。（新娘的兄弟）
chapters.en: Many waters cannot quench love, neither can the floods drown it: if man would give all the substance of his house for love, it would utterly be contemned.

chapters.zh: 我们有一小妹；他的两乳尚未长成，人来提亲的日子，我们当为他怎样办理？
chapters.en: We have a little sister, and she hath no breasts: what shall we do for our sister in the day when she shall be spoken for?

chapters.zh: 他若是墙，我们要在其上建造银塔；他若是门，我们要用香柏木板围护他。（新娘）
chapters.en: If she a wall, we will build upon her a palace of silver: and if she a door, we will inclose her with boards of cedar.

chapters.zh: 我是墙；我两乳像其上的楼。那时，我在他眼中像得平安的人。（新郎）
chapters.en: I a wall, and my breasts like towers: then was I in his eyes as one that found favour.

chapters.zh: 所罗门在巴力哈们有一葡萄园；他将这葡萄园交给看守的人，为其中的果子必交一千舍客勒银子。
chapters.en: Solomon had a vineyard at Baalhamon; he let out the vineyard unto keepers; every one for the fruit thereof was to bring a thousand of silver.

chapters.zh: 我自己的葡萄园在我面前。所罗门哪，一千舍客勒归你，二百舍客勒归看守果子的人。
chapters.en: My vineyard, which mine, before me: thou, O Solomon, a thousand, and those that keep the fruit thereof two hundred.

chapters.zh: 你这住在园中的，同伴都要听你的声音，求你使我也得听见。（新娘）
chapters.en: Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken to thy voice: cause me to hear .

chapters.zh: 我的良人哪，求你快来！如羚羊或小鹿在香草山上。
chapters.en: Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a roe or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices.
